stale_prose_check.note: no STALE row; 3 UNRESOLVABLE R3 rows in skills/review-and-fix/SKILL.md (informational, never gate)

checklist.claim: Loop-exit suite rule names a concrete durable cross-iteration tracking signal
checklist.evidence: The rule is prose-only ('Track that an UPDATED merge landed'); no field is named it reads. base_checkpoint was written only on the standalone arm; the implement-driven arm recorded outcomes as free-text workpad prose with no queryable field. Absent comparand -> fails open.

phase3_findings.description: Checkpoint-3 fence chains a bare `git push` after the helper, ungated by token: it pushes on CONFLICT (MERGE_HEAD present, branch behind base) and MERGE_IN_PROGRESS (hard-stop path).

PUSH_REJECTED's contract claims an unconditional restore the helper does not guarantee (_reject_restore has a failed-restore WARNING arm); the call site routes the token to 'non-fatal, continue' on that basis. Divergence lives in committed history, so no clean-tree backstop can see it.
item 7 bills itself 'the authoritative specification of every field the record carries' but omits base_checkpoint (introduced by item 6) — and likewise stale_prose_check.

'Track that an UPDATED merge landed this iteration' names no mechanism for the implement-driven arm; the Loop-exit suite rule's operand can be absent -> fails OPEN (suite silently does not re-run; a pushed base merge ships locally unverified). Corroborates Phase-2 FAIL VC-28.

phase-3-review.md self-contradicts within one paragraph: 'record-and-continue on UNVERIFIED/PUSH_REJECTED', then restricts proceeding to open the draft PR to only UP_TO_DATE/UPDATED/DISABLED — excluding the two outcomes just called non-fatal. A run hitting UNVERIFIED is wedged at Phase 3.1.
The dirty guard's untracked-files comment leans on a fail-closed behavioral claim (git refuses the merge before touching anything) that is deterministically constructible but untested.
guard-class-2 lint regex boundary class treats '/' as a non-boundary, so a path-qualified invocation (/usr/bin/head) would slip past the lint.

Four defense-in-depth arms of the helper remain untested (behind-by non-numeric case, post-unshallow keep-old-value fallback, _reject_restore failed-restore WARNING, _push_or_recover fetch-failure arm). Already self-disclosed as recorded deferrals in the diff.

fix_decisions.evidence: Re-affirmed at Important, not down-calibrated. Observable fail-direction is OPEN: the agent is told the tree is restored and continues; the divergence is in COMMITTED history so `git status` reads clean and Phase 4.3's clean-tree backstop cannot see it. No suite row catches it. A fail-open defect is graded on the direction it takes on the triggering input, not on how contrived that input is.

fix_decisions.claim_text: Ungated git push in the Checkpoint-3 fence
fix_decisions.evidence: Re-affirmed at Important. Fail-direction is OPEN: on CONFLICT the ungated push propagates a behind-base head (producing the very behind-base review deferral the feature exists to prevent) and on MERGE_IN_PROGRESS it pushes on a path that prescribes a hard stop. No guard downstream catches it.

fix_decisions.claim_text: Loop-exit suite rule operand has no named producer
fix_decisions.evidence: Re-affirmed at Important; corroborated 2/5 (silent-failure-hunter + Phase-2 FAIL VC-28). Fail-direction is OPEN: the suite silently does not re-run and a pushed base merge ships with no local verification. This is the documented unverified-assumption class (a guard whose comparand can be absent fails open exactly where it claims to fail closed).

fix_decisions.claim_text: guard-class-2 lint regex boundary class allows a path-qualified /usr/bin/head
fix_decisions.evidence: Real but low: the reporting agent itself graded it low and noted the helper never invokes these tools path-qualified. Tightening the boundary class risks false positives on legitimate tokens (refs/heads/, HEAD) that the current class deliberately excludes, so the change is not clearly-correct-and-trivial. Revisit if a path-qualified invocation ever appears in a helper.

Already recorded in the diff's own DEFERRED test gaps block with per-arm reasoning; each arm is fail-closed (resolves to UNVERIFIED/PUSH_REJECTED, never a silent success). Needs a git-stub seam to trigger deterministically. Revisit when such a seam is added.
